_metadata.note: All source paths are under C:/Users/johnn/Documents/Private-work/The Elites/Clients/TRD/TRD-content/. No two services share the same source image. Scores are out of 60 (6 criteria x 10 max).

hero.source: C:/Users/johnn/Documents/Private-work/The Elites/Clients/TRD/TRD-content/CARBON FIBRE REINFORCEMENT - CARINGBAH PAVILION/IMG_0671.JPG
hero.reason: Wide upward-looking shot of an entire large pavilion ceiling covered with a dramatic grid of black carbon-fibre reinforcement strips. A worker in hi-vis orange stands on a scissor lift providing scale. Excellent depth, industrial drama, and clear service identity.

feature.source: C:/Users/johnn/Documents/Private-work/The Elites/Clients/TRD/TRD-content/CARBON FIBRE REINFORCEMENT - CARINGBAH PAVILION/IMG_0685.JPG
feature.reason: Close-up of the completed CF strip cross-pattern on a concrete wall, showing the precision and craftsmanship of the bonded reinforcement layout. Clear product detail without clutter.

process.reason: Dramatic welding shot of a worker in orange hi-vis with welding shield; a cascade of bright orange sparks radiates across the frame against a dark background. Outstanding visual drama — the single best action shot in the entire library. Perfectly represents the structural steel reinforcement process.

hero.source: C:/Users/johnn/Documents/Private-work/The Elites/Clients/TRD/TRD-content/STRUCTURAL ALTERATIONS - FLORENCE AND CARPI WENTWORTHPOINT_/IMG_1436.JPG
hero.folder: STRUCTURAL ALTERATIONS - FLORENCE AND CARPI WENTWORTHPOINT_
hero.reason: Massive concrete panel suspended by chains while a worker in yellow hi-vis supervises; grinder sparks fly in the background. Entire scene framed by yellow temporary support props. Open sky above. Outstanding scale, drama, and authenticity. Best hero in the alterations set.

feature.source: C:/Users/johnn/Documents/Private-work/The Elites/Clients/TRD/TRD-content/STRUCTURAL ALTERATIONS - FLORENCE AND CARPI WENTWORTHPOINT_/IMG_1432.JPG
feature.folder: STRUCTURAL ALTERATIONS - FLORENCE AND CARPI WENTWORTHPOINT_
feature.reason: Cut concrete panel lifted by chain crane; worker directing the lift. Strong composition showcasing the precision required for structural alteration work. Clear feature-level detail of the key moment.

process.reason: Low-angle outdoor shot of a worker using a core drill on a concrete retaining wall, shot from below against a bright sky with city skyline visible. Excellent composition, dramatic lighting, strong colour contrast between orange hi-vis and blue sky. Unique outdoor setting distinguishes it from all other process shots.

hero.source: C:/Users/johnn/Documents/Private-work/The Elites/Clients/TRD/TRD-content/12C PELICAN ROAD SCHOFIELDS - SLAB ON GROUND CRACK INJECTION/IMG_3085.JPG
hero.folder: 12C PELICAN ROAD SCHOFIELDS - SLAB ON GROUND CRACK INJECTION
hero.reason: Wide horizontal shot of a long carpark wall entirely covered with crack injection treatment — multiple crack paths stitched with injection ports spanning the full width of the image. A green exit sign adds environmental context. Exceptional scale and documentary power. Perfect hero to establish service scope.

feature.source: C:/Users/johnn/Documents/Private-work/The Elites/Clients/TRD/TRD-content/STRUCTURAL WALL INJECTION - LOT 9 MARSDEN PARK/IMG_0928.JPG
feature.folder: STRUCTURAL WALL INJECTION - LOT 9 MARSDEN PARK
feature.reason: Abstract, almost artistic shot of a shoring wall covered with CFRP injection strips in sweeping curved branches across the full panel. The branching pattern is visually striking and communicates the complexity and coverage of a professional injection treatment.

process.source: C:/Users/johnn/Documents/Private-work/The Elites/Clients/TRD/TRD-content/16B PELICAN ROAD SCHOFIELDS - CRACK INJECTIONS/IMG_5386.JPG
process.reason: Extreme close-up of an injection port with tubing inserted directly into the crack, epoxy resin visible. Shows the exact technique of pressure injection in compelling detail. Best process shot in the crack-injection library.

hero.source: C:/Users/johnn/Documents/Private-work/The Elites/Clients/TRD/TRD-content/WALL CUTTING - FLORENCE & CAPRI WENTWORTHPOINT/IMG_5431.JPG
hero.reason: Outstanding hero: worker in lime-green hi-vis using a wall saw to cut through a concrete lift-well opening; set against a dramatic brooding Sydney skyline with heavy clouds. The framing through the opening creates a powerful picture-within-a-picture effect. Highest overall score in the entire library.

feature.source: C:/Users/johnn/Documents/Private-work/The Elites/Clients/TRD/TRD-content/WALL CUTTING - FLORENCE & CAPRI WENTWORTHPOINT/IMG_5433.JPG
feature.reason: Lower-angle shot of the same wall cutting job; shows the saw blade engaged and the full depth of cut visible. Good feature detail of equipment and technique. Used because the folder has only 3 images and the other two are allocated to hero and process roles.

process.source: C:/Users/johnn/Documents/Private-work/The Elites/Clients/TRD/TRD-content/WALL CUTTING - FLORENCE & CAPRI WENTWORTHPOINT/IMG_5432.JPG
process.reason: Slightly wider version of the hero shot with the same dramatic sky; worker fully visible in action. Works as the process shot showing the actual cutting operation in progress. Note: concrete-cutting folder has only video files or a scan readout as alternatives, so all 3 picks come from the same Florence & Capri folder — noted as a limitation.

hero.source: C:/Users/johnn/Documents/Private-work/The Elites/Clients/TRD/TRD-content/SHORING WALLA REPAIRS - PELICAN ROAD SCHOFIELDS/IMG_5880.JPG
hero.folder: SHORING WALLA REPAIRS - PELICAN ROAD SCHOFIELDS
hero.reason: Wide underground carpark shot showing a wall covered in dramatic branching crack repair patterns — black injection stitching contrasts against the sandy-coloured concrete wall under green-tinted emergency lighting. Ladder, buckets, and materials show active work. Strong visual atmosphere and excellent scope.

feature.reason: Dramatic macro of a row of injection ports on a dark rough concrete wall shot at low angle, creating a strong receding perspective. The metallic ports are crisp and clearly detailed. Almost photographic-art quality — best feature image in the concrete-repairs library.

process.source: C:/Users/johnn/Documents/Private-work/The Elites/Clients/TRD/TRD-content/16A PELICAN ROAD SCHOFIELDS - CONCRETE CRACK REPAIR/IMG_5288.JPG
process.folder: 16A PELICAN ROAD SCHOFIELDS - CONCRETE CRACK REPAIR
process.reason: Overhead view of a worker with a core drill in a tight stitch-drilling pattern around a concrete opening. The circular sequence of drill holes is visually striking and clearly shows the technique used for structural repair. Excellent process documentation.

hero.source: C:/Users/johnn/Documents/Private-work/The Elites/Clients/TRD/TRD-content/SLAB SCANNING - POST-TENSION TRUNCATION - ROUSEHILL/IMG_5814.JPG
hero.reason: Wide shot of a technician kneeling over a GPR scanner on a slab, with vivid blue paint-marked scan lines clearly visible. Metal stud framing in background gives industrial context. Good composition with warm vs cool colour contrast. Largest file in slab-scanning set.

feature.source: C:/Users/johnn/Documents/Private-work/The Elites/Clients/TRD/TRD-content/SLAB SCANNING - POST-TENSION TRUNCATION - ROUSEHILL/IMG_5812.JPG
feature.reason: Close-up view of the GPR scanner device being operated; the scanning unit, tablet, and marked slab are all clearly visible. Best image for showcasing the technology and equipment used in slab scanning.

process.source: C:/Users/johnn/Documents/Private-work/The Elites/Clients/TRD/TRD-content/SLAB SCANNING - POST-TENSION TRUNCATION - ROUSEHILL/IMG_5820.JPG
process.reason: Technician in orange hi-vis actively pushing the GPR scanner unit with real-time readout visible on the tablet. Blue scan marks on the slab show previous passes. Strong action and process narrative.

hero.reason: Wide-angle perspective along a long brick wall with injection ports marked at regular intervals in two columns. A worker in yellow hi-vis crouches at the base actively working. The receding perspective down the wall is dramatic and gives excellent depth for a hero image.

feature.reason: Close-up looking up at a brick wall densely covered with injection ports in a systematic grid. The repeating geometric pattern clearly showcases the thoroughness and methodology of curtain wall injection treatment.

process.reason: Worker actively crouched at the base of the brick wall during the injection process; injection equipment and hoses visible. Shows the technique in situ with strong contextual atmosphere.

hero.reason: Aerial perspective of a long run of vibrant orange Surelock joint formers installed in a straight line on black waterproofing membrane; construction site with trees and blue sky in background. Striking colour contrast and strong perspective depth. Best hero composition in the joints library.

feature.reason: Close-up of Surelock joint formers attached to formwork, showing the product detail including the ribbed orange former, reinforcement bars, and waterproofing membrane. Best feature image to showcase the system.

process.reason: Close-up of liquid sealant being poured/applied into a freshly cut joint channel in a concrete slab; shows the active repair process. The wet black sealant against grey concrete with wood-form edges is visually distinct and clearly illustrates the technique.

hero.source: C:/Users/johnn/Documents/Private-work/The Elites/Clients/TRD/TRD-content/SLAB SCANNING - POST-TENSION TRUNCATION - ROUSEHILL/IMG_5818.JPG
hero.reason: Wide shot of GPR scanning technician kneeling over scanner with blue scan-line markings visible on the slab; stud framing of new construction in background. Establishes the scanning phase of post-tension truncation work. Distinct from slab-scanning hero because this one more clearly shows the scanning-as-preparation context.

feature.source: C:/Users/johnn/Documents/Private-work/The Elites/Clients/TRD/TRD-content/SLAB SCANNING - POST-TENSION TRUNCATION - ROUSEHILL/PHOTO-2026-02-17-20-16-04 4.jpg
feature.reason: Overhead view of multiple PT cable trenches cut in a cross-pattern through the slab; bundles of exposed steel strands, rebar starters, and grout cups clearly visible. Highly technical and visually distinctive — the only image in the library that unambiguously shows post-tension cables exposed during truncation work.

process.source: C:/Users/johnn/Documents/Private-work/The Elites/Clients/TRD/TRD-content/SLAB SCANNING - POST-TENSION TRUNCATION - ROUSEHILL/IMG_5906.JPG
process.reason: Worker pouring white grout from a bucket into a cut PT cable trench; the trench with exposed cables is visible below. Clear action shot showing the rectification/grouting step of the post-tension truncation repair process.
